samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_8bit">
    <description>Implement an 8-bit unsigned multiplier using pipelined processing.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="mul_en_in">Input enable signal for multiplication.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplicand input.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplier input.</input>
        <output id="mul_en_out">Output enable signal for multiplication.</output>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[15:0]">Product output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="IDLE">0</constant>
        <constant id="LOAD">1</constant>
        <constant id="CALCULATE">2</constant>
        <constant id="OUTPUT">3</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="mul_en_out_reg" width_description="[3:0]">Register to hold the output enable signal.</reg>
        <reg id="state" width_description="[1:0]">State variable for FSM.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" width_description="[1:0]">Next state variable for FSM.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_a_reg" width_description="[7:0]">Register to hold the multiplicand.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_b_reg" width_description="[7:0]">Register to hold the multiplier.</reg>
        <wire id="temp[0]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold temporary partial product.</wire>
        <wire id="temp[1]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold temporary partial product.</wire>
        <wire id="temp[2]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold temporary partial product.</wire>
        <wire id="temp[3]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold temporary partial product.</wire>
        <wire id="temp[4]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold temporary partial product.</wire>
        <wire id="temp[5]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold temporary partial product.</wire>
        <wire id="temp[6]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold temporary partial product.</wire>
        <wire id="temp[7]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold temporary partial product.</wire>
        <reg id="sum[0]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the first partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[1]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the second partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[2]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the third partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[3]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the fourth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[4]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the fifth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[5]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the sixth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_out_reg" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the final product.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Sequential logic block triggered on positive edge of the clock
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                // Reset condition: Set state to IDLE
                state <= IDLE;
            end else begin
                // Update state to next_state on positive edge of clock
                state <= next_state;
            end
        end
        
        // Combinational logic block to determine next state
        always @(*) begin
            case(state)
                IDLE: begin
                    // If input enable is low, stay in IDLE state
                    if(~mul_en_in) begin
                        next_state = IDLE;
                    end else begin
                        // If input enable is high, move to LOAD state
                        next_state = LOAD;
                    end
                end
                
                LOAD: begin
                    // Move to CALCULATE state after loading inputs
                    next_state = CALCULATE;
                end
                
                CALCULATE: begin
                    // After calculating, move to OUTPUT state
                    next_state = OUTPUT;
                end
                
                OUTPUT: begin
                    // Return to IDLE state after outputting result
                    next_state = IDLE;
                end
                
                default: begin
                    // Default case: Stay in IDLE state
                    next_state = IDLE;
                end
            endcase
        end
        
        // Always block triggered on positive edge of clock
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                // Reset condition: Clear registers
                mul_a_reg <= 8'd0;
                mul_b_reg <= 8'd0;
                mul_en_out_reg <= 4'd0;
            end else begin
                // Load inputs into registers based on state
                case(state)
                    IDLE: begin
                        if(mul_en_in) begin
                            mul_a_reg <= mul_a;
                            mul_b_reg <= mul_b;
                        end
                    end
                    
                    LOAD: begin
                        // No action needed in LOAD state
                    end
                    
                    CALCULATE: begin
                        // Perform calculations based on current state
                        mul_en_out_reg <= {3'd0, mul_en_in};
                    end
                    
                    OUTPUT: begin
                        // No action needed in OUTPUT state
                    end
                    
                    default: begin
                        // Default case: No action
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
        
        // Generate partial products based on multiplier bits
        genvar i;
        generate
            for(i = 0; i < 8; i = i + 1) begin : pp_gen
                assign temp[i][15:0] = (mul_b_reg[i]) ? {{8{1'b0}}, mul_a_reg} << i : 16'd0;
            end
        endgenerate
        
        // Calculate partial sums based on generated partial products
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                sum[0] <= 16'd0;
                sum[1] <= 16'd0;
                sum[2] <= 16'd0;
                sum[3] <= 16'd0;
                sum[4] <= 16'd0;
                sum[5] <= 16'd0;
            end else begin
                case(state)
                    IDLE: begin
                        // No action needed in IDLE state
                    end
                    
                    LOAD: begin
                        // No action needed in LOAD state
                    end
                    
                    CALCULATE: begin
                        // Calculate partial sums based on generated partial products
                        sum[0] <= temp[0];
                        sum[1] <= temp[1] + temp[2];
                        sum[2] <= temp[3] + temp[4];
                        sum[3] <= temp[5] + temp[6];
                        sum[4] <= temp[7];
                        sum[5] <= 16'd0;
                    end
                    
                    OUTPUT: begin
                        // No action needed in OUTPUT state
                    end
                    
                    default: begin
                        // Default case: No action
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
        
        // Calculate final product based on calculated partial sums
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                mul_out_reg <= 16'd0;
            end else begin
                case(state)
                    IDLE: begin
                        // No action needed in IDLE state
                    end
                    
                    LOAD: begin
                        // No action needed in LOAD state
                    end
                    
                    CALCULATE: begin
                        // No action needed in CALCULATE state
                    end
                    
                    OUTPUT: begin
                        // Accumulate partial sums to get final product
                        mul_out_reg <= sum[0] + sum[1] + sum[2] + sum[3] + sum[4] + sum[5];
                    end
                    
                    default: begin
                        // Default case: No action
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
        
        // Assign output based on output enable signal
        assign mul_out = (mul_en_out_reg[3]) ? mul_out_reg : 16'd0;
        
        // Assign output enable signal
        assign mul_en_out = mul_en_out_reg[3];
    </implementation>
    
endmodule
